articles.title: The Book of Mormon. S. T. Brimhall-Foley
articles.complete_text:
The Book of Mormon
By S. T. Brimhall-Foley

Flower from the hillside,
Sweet September rose,
Planted by prophets
In soil that they chose.

Silent for ages,
At length to come forth
And speak as by magic
Through tongues of the earth.

Rose of Columbia
No hand shall destroy,
Plucked by an angel
And read by a boy.

Flower of a nation
And light to a world,
With fragrance unceasing
Its bright leaves unfold.

The plant of its fountain
Shall ever increase,
Be guarded by angels
Till earth rests in peace.
articles.author: S. T. Brimhall-Foley
articles.notes: Found the complete poem on page 463. It's a 5-stanza poem about the Book of Mormon, using metaphors of flowers and roses to describe the sacred text.
